name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
description: An initiative by the Government of India that provides up to INR 6,000 per year in three equal installments to all landholding farmer families.
benefit: INR 6,000 per year paid in three equal installments of INR 2,000 directly to the bank account.

eligibility.rules: Must own cultivable land. Excludes institutional landholders, taxpayers, and government employees.

name: Ayushman Bharat (PM-JAY)
description: The largest health assurance scheme in the world, providing health cover up to INR 5 Lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
benefit: Cashless health cover up to INR 5,00,000 per family per year at all empaneled hospitals.

eligibility.rules: Must be listed in the SECC BPL list or live in a kutcha house with low annual income (< INR 1.8 Lakhs).

name: PMAY-G (Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin)
description: A social welfare program to provide financial assistance to rural households living in kutcha or dilapidated houses to construct a pucca house.
benefit: Financial assistance of INR 1,20,000 in plain areas and INR 1,30,000 in hilly/difficult areas for house construction.

eligibility.rules: Must not own any pucca house in India. The family must live in a kutcha house with 0, 1, or 2 rooms.

documents: Job Card / Swachh Bharat Mission ID
Affidavit stating non-ownership of pucca house

name: PM Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
description: A scheme to provide clean cooking fuel (LPG connections) to women from below poverty line (BPL) households, replacing unhealthy traditional biomass fuel.
benefit: Free LPG gas connection, financial support of INR 1,600, and first 14.2 kg stove cylinder free of charge.

eligibility.rules: Must be a female applicant above 18 years, residing in a BPL household, with no existing LPG connection in the family.

documents: Aadhaar Card of Applicant and adult family members
BPL Ration Card (or state-equivalent BPL list)

name: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee)
description: A social security measure that guarantees 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to a rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
benefit: Guaranteed 100 days of paid manual work per year at state-specific minimum wages.

eligibility.rules: Must be an adult member of a rural household willing to perform unskilled physical manual labor.

documents: Recent passport size photographs (for Job Card)

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
description: A small deposit savings scheme promoted under the Beti Bachao Beti Padhao campaign, offering high interest rates and tax benefits for the girl child's education/marriage.
benefit: Tax-exempt savings account with a high compound interest rate (currently 8.2%) for the girl child's future.

eligibility.rules: The account can be opened only in the name of a girl child who is less than 10 years of age. Maximum of 2 accounts per family.

documents: Birth Certificate of the Girl Child
Aadhaar Card of the Parent / Guardian
PAN Card of the Parent / Guardian
Address Proof of the Parent / Guardian

name: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
description: A government-backed accident insurance scheme in India providing coverage for accidental death and disability.
benefit: Insurance coverage of INR 2 Lakhs for accidental death or total permanent disability, and INR 1 Lakh for partial permanent disability.

eligibility.rules: Must have an active savings bank account. The premium of INR 20 per year is auto-debited annually.

name: PM-SYM (Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan)
description: A pension scheme for unorganized workers like street vendors, rickshaw pullers, construction workers, ragpickers, and gig workers to secure their old age.
benefit: Assured minimum monthly pension of INR 3,000 after attaining the age of 60 years.

eligibility.rules: Must be an unorganized worker aged 18 to 40 with a monthly income under INR 15,000 (INR 1.8 Lakhs/year) and not covered under EPF/ESIC/NPS.

name: PM Kisan Maan-dhan Yojana (PM-KMY)
description: An old-age pension scheme for all small and marginal landholding farmers to ensure social security in their senior years.
benefit: Minimum assured pension of INR 3,000 per month after completing 60 years of age.

eligibility.rules: Must be a small and marginal farmer aged 18 to 40, owning cultivable land up to 2 hectares (approx. 5 acres).
